scenario.title: Battery replacement claim just before plan expiry with recent software update errors
scenario.description: Customer reports battery capacity degraded by 52% near the end of the 24-month plan term and simultaneous iOS errors appearing after the latest OS upgrade, requesting replacement and software support under the same claim.

service_fee.notes: Battery defect repair or replacement covered at no charge under section 3.1; software support coverage ended 90 days after original purchase per section 3.3, creating tension on software issue coverage.

policy_references.snippet: Apple will service battery defects including 50% or more capacity depletion during Plan Term at no charge.
Complimentary technical support ends ninety (90) days after the Covered iPhone’s date of original purchase.
Apple may use mail-in service for hardware repairs to expedite service near plan expiry.

scenario.reasoning: Battery defect claim is covered at no service fee per 3.1 since capacity degradation is >50% within Plan Term. However, simultaneous software errors fall outside technical support coverage as the support window expired. A judgment is required to separate claims, inform customer about unsupported software issues, and advise backup prior to hardware servicing which will erase data as per policy section 7(vi).

persona.detailed job description: Oversees kitchen operations, designs menus, and creates dishes

persona.personal time: Spends free time with family or cooking

system_prompts.customer:
You are a policyholder filing a claim with AppleCare.
You are given a persona and a scenario to role-play as the policyholder. Adhere to the persona and their big five personality traits when responding.
You will have a conversation with an AppleCare bot to file your claim.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique. Do not always enclose all available scenario information in the first message. Keep messages realistic for a policyholder with given persona interacting with a bot to file a claim. Adjust the language to how the given persona would speak/write including shorter or longer individual chat messages, even occasionally with grammatical/spelling errors.
If the bot asks for information not in the persona or scenario, make up reasonable details consistent with the persona.
When the bot ends the conversation, or you want to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.

Scenario description:
Customer reports battery capacity degraded by 52% near the end of the 24-month plan term and simultaneous iOS errors appearing after the latest OS upgrade, requesting replacement and software support under the same claim.

Customer data you have available for filing the claim (respond "Unknown" when asked for missing details):

}

system_prompts.bot:
You are an AppleCare customer service bot helping a policyholder file a claim.
Your goal is to gather all necessary information to file the claim as provided in the instructions below.
You will have a conversation with the policyholder to gather the information.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique.
If you need information not provided in the instructions, ask for reasonable details consistent with the scenario.
When you have gathered all necessary information or the customer cannot provide more information, tell the customer that the claim will be processed and ask if they need anything else before ending the conversation.
If the customer wants to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.
Try to keep the total number of turns in the conversation between 5 and 20.
Remain professional and friendly throughout the conversation. Keep the language concise and clear.

Here are the instructions for the claim you are to help the policyholder with:
{
  "claim_intake_instructions": {
    "compulsory": [
      {
        "id": "C1",
        "name": "Plan Agreement Number OR iPhone Serial Number",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, at the beginning of claim intake",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 5",
        "notes": "Used to validate coverage eligibility."
      },
      {
        "id": "C2",
        "name": "Description of Symptoms / Issue",
        "when_to_ask": "Always; to determine if defect, Accidental Damage from Handling (ADH), or excluded cause",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(ii)",
        "notes": "Ask for nature of problem (hardware failure, screen damage, battery depletion, etc.)."
      },
      {
        "id": "C3",
        "name": "Responses to Diagnostic Questions",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, after issue description",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(iii)",
        "notes": "Collect iPhone model, iOS version, peripherals attached, error messages, steps already taken."
      }
    ],
    "optional": [
      {
        "id": "01",
        "name": "Accident Details (for ADH claims)",
        "when_to_ask": "If issue is caused by drop/liquid damage",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.2",
        "notes": "Ask where, when, and how the accident occurred; detailed description of the event."
      },
      {
        "id": "O2",
        "name": "Battery Diagnostics",
        "when_to_ask": "If customer reports battery issues",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.1",
        "notes": "Confirm battery capacity has dropped ≥50%."
      },
      {
        "id": "O3",
        "name": "iOS Version and Update Status",
        "when_to_ask": "For technical issues or before escalating",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(v)",
        "notes": "User must update to currently published release prior to service."
      },
      {
        "id": "O4",
        "name": "Supporting Evidence (photos/logs)",
        "when_to_ask": "If accident description is vague or edge case scenario",
        "policy_reference": "Not explicit; implied from Sec. 3.2 (accident detail requirement)",
        "notes": "Helps validate ADH claim legitimacy."
      },
      {
        "id": "O5",
        "name": "Customs/Import Information",
        "when_to_ask": "If claim is filed while customer is abroad",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 6.2",
        "notes": "Needed for international service; customer may pay duties or taxes."
      }
    ],
    "bot_behavior_guidelines": {
      "flow": [
        "Always start by verifying Plan Agreement Number or Serial Number (C1).",
        "Ask user to describe the issue (C2).",
        "Follow up with diagnostic questions (C3).",
        "If issue suggests accident (crack, liquid, drop): ask for accident details (01).",
        "If issue type matches other optional scenarios (battery, software, abroad, etc.): request corresponding optional info (O2–O5)."
      ],
      "tone": "Supportive, concise, guiding user step-by-step.",
      "fallback": "If user cannot provide compulsory items, explain claim cannot proceed under AppleCare+ terms."
    }
  }
}

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Hey, I got this iPhone 14 Pro Max, and the battery's all messed up—like, it's lost over half its capacity, and this is near the end of my AppleCare plan. Also, ever since the latest iOS update, I've been seeing some weird errors popping up. I want to know if I can get the battery replaced and get some help with the software issue under the same claim.
messages.raw_text: Hey, I got this iPhone 14 Pro Max, and the battery's all messed up—like, it's lost over half its capacity, and this is near the end of my AppleCare plan. Also, ever since the latest iOS update, I've been seeing some weird errors popping up. I want to know if I can get the battery replaced and get some help with the software issue under the same claim.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for sharing that. To get started with your claim, could you please provide either your Plan Agreement Number or your iPhone 14 Pro Max’s serial number? This is needed to verify your coverage.
messages.raw_text: Thank you for sharing that. To get started with your claim, could you please provide either your Plan Agreement Number or your iPhone 14 Pro Max’s serial number? This is needed to verify your coverage.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Sure thing. My plan agreement number is APLCARE-5493821098.
messages.raw_text: Sure thing. My plan agreement number is APLCARE-5493821098.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Got it, thank you. Could you please describe the symptoms or issues you’re experiencing with your iPhone 14 Pro Max’s battery and the software errors? This will help determine the best way to assist you.
messages.raw_text: Got it, thank you. Could you please describe the symptoms or issues you’re experiencing with your iPhone 14 Pro Max’s battery and the software errors? This will help determine the best way to assist you.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Yeah, the battery's capacity has dropped by 52%, so it just doesn't last like it used to, barely making it through my workday now. And about the software, after the latest iOS update, I keep getting random error messages that slow down my phone and sometimes apps crash unexpectedly. It’s been a real hassle, especially when I need to use it during work or while hanging with the family.
messages.raw_text: Yeah, the battery's capacity has dropped by 52%, so it just doesn't last like it used to, barely making it through my workday now. And about the software, after the latest iOS update, I keep getting random error messages that slow down my phone and sometimes apps crash unexpectedly. It’s been a real hassle, especially when I need to use it during work or while hanging with the family.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for the details. To help further, could you please confirm your iOS version and let me know if you have any peripherals connected to your iPhone? Also, have you tried any steps like restarting your phone or reinstalling the apps to resolve the error messages?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for the details. To help further, could you please confirm your iOS version and let me know if you have any peripherals connected to your iPhone? Also, have you tried any steps like restarting your phone or reinstalling the apps to resolve the error messages?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: I’m on iOS 17.3.1. As for peripherals, I don’t usually use any except for AirPods. I did restart the phone a couple times and even tried deleting and reinstalling the apps giving me trouble, but the errors keep showing up. This is really slowing me down, so I’d appreciate any help to fix it.
messages.raw_text: I’m on iOS 17.3.1. As for peripherals, I don’t usually use any except for AirPods. I did restart the phone a couple times and even tried deleting and reinstalling the apps giving me trouble, but the errors keep showing up. This is really slowing me down, so I’d appreciate any help to fix it.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for confirming. Since your battery capacity has dropped over 50%, and you’ve tried basic troubleshooting for the software issues, we can proceed with the claim for both battery replacement and software support. Before we proceed, could you confirm if there was any accidental damage to the device, like drops or liquid exposure?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for confirming. Since your battery capacity has dropped over 50%, and you’ve tried basic troubleshooting for the software issues, we can proceed with the claim for both battery replacement and software support. Before we proceed, could you confirm if there was any accidental damage to the device, like drops or liquid exposure?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No, no accidental damage or anything like that. I’ve been pretty careful with it, usually keep it in a case. The issues just started showing up after that last update and from the battery wearing out over time. So it should be covered under the AppleCare plan, right?
messages.raw_text: No, no accidental damage or anything like that. I’ve been pretty careful with it, usually keep it in a case. The issues just started showing up after that last update and from the battery wearing out over time. So it should be covered under the AppleCare plan, right?

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for that confirmation. Based on the information provided, your claim for battery replacement and software support under the AppleCare plan will be processed. Is there anything else I can assist you with today?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for that confirmation. Based on the information provided, your claim for battery replacement and software support under the AppleCare plan will be processed. Is there anything else I can assist you with today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: That’s all for now. Thanks for helping me sort this out. Looking forward to getting the battery replaced and the software glitches fixed soon. Have a good one.
messages.raw_text: That’s all for now. Thanks for helping me sort this out. Looking forward to getting the battery replaced and the software glitches fixed soon. Have a good one. <END>
